columns.name: OUTPAT_DUP_INT_STR
columns.description: Indicate the lookback time for duplicate checking. This item stores the string value of the duplicate interval (in days). This value is converted into hours and populated into item-17210 Outpatient Duplicate Interval. To specify an interval in DAYS, you can enter a positive number. To specify an interval in HOURS, you can enter it in terms of days. (e.g., to specify 6 hours, enter .25) To specify the interval in weeks, you can enter a number/fraction followed by the letter W. (e.g., to specify 3 weeks, type 3W or 3.0W). The value is converted to equivalent days and displayed to the user. Note: Large interval values may cause the system to slow down considerably. Please consult your system Technical Services Representative before setting large values in this field. When a procedure is ordered, a check is made to see if that procedure had been previously ordered within the duplicate interval. If found, the user is asked whether to continue placing the order. The order in which duplicate interval is looked up is - Procedure -> Procedure Category -> Department -> Misc. configuration If the interval value in the Misc. Configuration level is used and if the interval is empty or equal to zero, the system will check duplicates against orders placed in the same session; if the interval is -1, the duplicate check will be disabled

columns.name: INPAT_DUP_INT_STR
columns.description: Indicate the lookback time for duplicate checking. This item stores the string value of the duplicate interval (in days). This value is converted into hours and populated into item-17220 Inpatient Duplicate Interval. To specify an interval in DAYS, you can enter a positive number. To specify an interval in HOURS, you can enter it in terms of days. (e.g., to specify 6 hours, enter .25) To specify the interval in weeks, you can enter a number/fraction followed by the letter W. (e.g., to specify 3 weeks, type 3W or 3.0W). The value is converted to equivalent days and displayed to the user. Note: Large interval values may cause the system to slow down considerably. Please consult your system Technical Services Representative before setting large values in this field. When a procedure is ordered, a check is made to see if that procedure had been previously ordered within the duplicate interval. If found, the user is asked whether to continue placing the order. The order in which duplicate interval is looked up is -Procedure -> Procedure Category -> Department -> Misc. configuration If the interval value in the Misc. Configuration level is used and if the interval is empty or equal to zero, the system will check duplicates against orders placed in the same session; if the interval is -1, the duplicate check will be disabled.

columns.name: DEF_DIVISION_C

columns.name: PROV_START_DATE

columns.name: PRACTICE_NAME_C

columns.name: SURG_EQP_SVCDAT_YN
columns.description: Stores whether the surgical equipment should be serviced on a particular date.

columns.name: SURG_COST_TBL_ID
columns.description: Stores the cost table ID (OCT) for the surgical staff/resource.

columns.name: TEAM_LEADER_ID
columns.description: This column contains the provider ID (SER dot one) of the provider's team leader.

columns.name: TEAM_C
columns.description: This column contains the team that the provider is assigned to.

columns.name: SUP_PROV_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this provider is a supervising provider.

columns.name: EMPLOYED_CRNA_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the provider is a Certified Registered Nurse Anesthetist.

columns.name: IS_INTERPRETER_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the provider is an interpreter.

columns.name: DOCTORS_DEGREE
columns.description: The degrees, such as M.D., held by this provider.

columns.name: REVENUE_DEPT_ID
columns.description: This is the default department for which revenue generated by this provider should be associated. Currently, this field is only used for determining the correct service area in which to create an HPF encounter when ADT triggers encounter creation.

columns.name: ENC_PROV_YN
columns.description: Determines whether the SER record can be used as a default encounter provider when creating quick encounters in the ambulatory EMR. Null is treated as yes.

columns.name: PHARMACIST_YN
columns.description: Is this provider a pharmacist?

columns.name: LAB_FAX_NUMBER
columns.description: Stores a fax number that will be used to route paper result reports from the Lab.

columns.name: PROV_PHOTO
columns.description: Path to the provider photo to use in the Welcome kiosk.

columns.name: USE_DEPT_VT_LIM_YN
columns.description: In table CLARITY_SER, the column USE_DEPT_VT_LIM_YN (SER/160) has been deprecated. This column has been replaced by column DEPT_VT_LMT_DEPT_YN (SER/161) in table CLARITY_SER_DEPT. Unlike this column, the replacement column is department specific. That is, each row in CLARITY_SER_DEPT will contain information on whether the provider in a specific department uses department visit type limits.

columns.name: VERIFYING_PERSON_ID
columns.description: User ID of the person verifying the SER record.

columns.name: OR_VLD_DT_OFST
columns.description: Release offset - How many months into the future this resource can be scheduled in OpTime.

columns.name: OR_CHARGE_CODE_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the charge code associated with this equipment resource.

columns.name: DIRECTORY_INFO
columns.description: Contains extended information for display in a facility directory in the Welcome kiosk.

columns.name: DBC_EXT_POS_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the EAF record that represents the external place of service for the provider. It is only for providers deemed external by the internal/external flag (I SER 190).

columns.name: RES_POOL_TYPE_C
columns.description: The resource pool type category number for this resource.

columns.name: EDI_CLM_ACTIVE_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether EDI Claims Eligibility table is active. The default is no.

columns.name: PROV_CLM_PROC_STA_C
columns.description: The category number indicating whether claim processing will send claims to a separate run for this provider for some payors or plans.

columns.name: PAYEE_NUM_DEFAULT
columns.description: The payee code which should appear by default in box 33 of Medicaid Claims using standard form 182 (Illinois Medicaid).

columns.name: SER_CLM_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider for custom claims.

columns.name: MCD_PROF_CD_C
columns.description: The NYSDOH Profession Code category number for the provider. This is used for New York Medicaid.

columns.name: OP_ORD_PROV_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the provider is an outpatient ordering provider. A value of Yes means that the provider is an outpatient ordering provider, and is the default value. A value of No means that the provider is not an outpatient ordering provider.

columns.name: IS_SUP_PROV_REQ_C
columns.description: The category value stating if supervision is required for this provider. A value of 3-Yes means that supervision is required; a value of 1-No means that supervision is not allowed or required. A value of 2-Optional means that supervision is allowed, but not required.

columns.name: PROVIDER_TYPE_C
columns.description: The provider type category number for the provider or resource. This is extracted as the category ID.

columns.name: EPRESCRIBING_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether a provider is activated to e-prescribe. A null value indicates that the provider has not yet been activated.

columns.name: EP_FLAG_YN
columns.description: In table CLARITY_SER, the column EP_FLAG_YN (SER/12299) has been deprecated. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles. Information about whether a SER record is considered an eligible professional can be found in the MU_EP_PROV_STATUS table.

columns.name: SEX_C
columns.description: The category number corresponding to the sex of the provider.

columns.name: ACTIVE_STATUS_C
columns.description: This item contains the status for the record. This is extracted as the category ID and represents the same data as ACTIVE_STATUS. This is the preferred column because it extracts the ID.

columns.name: REFERRAL_SOURCE_TYPE_C
columns.description: The referral source type for the provider. This indicates whether the provider is an internal or external source provider. This is extracted as the category ID and represents the same data as REFERRAL_SOURCE_TYPE. This is the preferred column because it extracts the ID.

columns.name: STAFF_RESOURCE_C
columns.description: Indicates whether the provider record is for a person, resource, or class. This is extracted as the category ID and represents the same data as STAFF_RESOURCE. This is the preferred column because it extracts the ID.

columns.name: REFERRAL_SRCE_TYPE_C
columns.description: The referral source type for the provider or resource. This indicates whether the referral source is a person, media, or other source. This is extracted as the category ID and represents the same data as REFERRAL_SRCE_TYPE. This is the preferred column because it extracts the ID. To get the category title use ZC_REF_PROV_TYPE.

columns.name: _SURROGATEKEY
columns.description: Surrogate key used to uniquely identify rows in this (and only this) table on Cogito Cloud only, do not assume the surrogate key in this table will correspond to the surrogate key in another table

tables.description: The ORDER_MED table enables you to report on medications ordered in EpicCare (prescriptions). We have also included patient and contact identification information for each record.

columns.name: ORDER_MED_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the order record associated with this medication order. This is an internal unique identifier for ORD master file records in this table and cannot be used to link to CLARITY_MEDICATION.

columns.name: PAT_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the patient record for this line. This column is frequently used to link to the PATIENT table.

columns.name: PAT_ENC_DATE_REAL
columns.description: A unique, internal contact date in decimal format. The integer portion of the number indicates the date of the contact. The digits after the decimal distinguish different contacts on the same date and are unique for each contact on that date. For example, .00 is the first/only contact, .01 is the second contact, etc.

columns.name: PAT_ENC_CSN_ID
columns.description: The unique contact serial number (CSN) for the patient contact associated with this medication order. This number is unique across patients and encounters in your system. If you use IntraConnect, this is the Unique Contact Identifier (UCI).

columns.name: ORDERING_DATE
columns.description: The date when the medication order was placed.

columns.name: ORDER_CLASS_C
columns.description: The category number for the order class. This value is used to define how clinical systems process the order.

columns.name: PHARMACY_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the pharmacy record that is associated with this medication order. This column is frequently used to link to the RX_PHR table. This field is only populated if the clinical system user selects a specific pharmacy from the list, otherwise the field is null. This field is only populated by the ambulatory clinical system, not the pharmacy system.

columns.name: COSIGNER_USER_ID
columns.description: In table ORDER_MED, the column COSIGNER_USER_ID has been deprecated. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for cosign information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: ORD_CREATR_USER_ID
columns.description: The EMP ID (.1) of the user who signed the order (for a non-signed and held order) or the last person who performed a sign and hold or release action for a signed and held order.

columns.name: MEDICATION_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the medication record that is associated with this order. In some circumstances, for example when Intelligent Medication Selection selects an IMS mixture, this column may contain template records that do not represent real medications. For this reason, it is recommended to use the Clarity column ORDER_MEDINFO.DISPENSABLE_MED_ID when reporting on medication orders.

columns.name: DESCRIPTION
columns.description: The description of the order. This information is found in the Order field of clinical system?s Order Detail window.

columns.name: SIG
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** In table ORDER_MED, the column SIG has been deprecated. This column has been replaced by column SIG_TEXT in the table ORDER_MED_SIG. To look up the deprecated column's value after the Clarity Compass upgrade, use the column SIG_TEXT value. The SIG_TEXT column has been expanded to hold more characters in the new table ORDER_MED_SIG.

columns.name: DOSAGE
columns.description: The dispensation amount for the prescription entered by the user in the orders activity. This amount is stored as a string in the orders database.

columns.name: QUANTITY
columns.description: The quantity of the prescription being dispensed as entered by the user.

columns.name: REFILLS
columns.description: The number of refills allowed for this prescription as entered by the user.

columns.name: START_DATE
columns.description: The date when the medication order started. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: END_DATE
columns.description: The date when the medication order is to end.

columns.name: DISP_AS_WRITTEN_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether or not the prescription should be dispensed as written for this medication.

columns.name: RSN_FOR_DISCON_C
columns.description: The category number for the reason a prescription has been discontinued. This column contains data only in prescription orders that have been discontinued.

columns.name: MED_PRESC_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider who has prescribed or authorized the medication order. The value in this column matches the value in the AUTHRZING_PROV_ID column.

columns.name: NONFRM_XCPT_CD_C
columns.description: The category number for medication's exception code. This code explains the reason a non-formulary medication was ordered.

columns.name: PANEL_MED_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the medication panel that is associated with this medication order. This column is only populated if the medication order was originally placed as part of a panel.

columns.name: SERV_AREA_ID
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** In table ORDER_MED, the column SERV_AREA_ID has been deprecated. This column has been replaced by column SERV_AREA_ID in table PAT_ENC. Please reference the replacement column to get the relevant values.

columns.name: UPDATE_DATE
columns.description: The date and time when this row was created or last updated in Clarity.

columns.name: ORDER_INST
columns.description: The date and time the order was placed. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: DISPLAY_NAME
columns.description: The name of the medication as it appears on the medication record itself.

columns.name: AS_MEDICATION_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the brand name medication originally chosen by the ordering user. This column is blank if the user did not chose a brand name record. It is recommended to use the Clarity column ORDER_MEDINFO.DISPENSABLE_MED_ID when reporting on medication orders. Use AS_MEDICATION_ID if specifically searching for orders that were originally selected from a preference list as a brand name medication.

columns.name: HV_HOSPITALIST_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this is a hospitalist order. A Y indicates a hospitalist order.

columns.name: PROV_STATUS
columns.description: This column is deprecated. The column previously extracted the category title. In order to avoid data consistency issues, use PROV_STATUS_C to link to ZC_PROV_STATUS instead.

columns.name: ORDER_PRIORITY_C
columns.description: The category number for the priority assigned to an order.

columns.name: COSIGN_AUTH_TIME
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for cosign information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: COSIGN_USER_ID
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for cosign information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: MED_ROUTE_C
columns.description: The category number for the route of administration of a medication.

columns.name: DISCON_USER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the user who discontinued the order.

columns.name: DISCON_TIME
columns.description: The date and time when the medication order was discontinued. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: CHNG_ORDER_MED_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the changed or reordered medication order that this order replaced. This column is frequently used to link back to the ORDER_MED table.

columns.name: PEND_APPR_USER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the user who approved a pended order.

columns.name: PEND_APPR_FLAG
columns.description: This column is deprecated. The column previously extracted the category title. In order to avoid data consistency issues, use PEND_APPROVE_FLAG_C to link to ZC_PEND_APPROVE_FL instead.

columns.name: PEND_REF_REAS_C
columns.description: The category number for the reason a pended medication was refused.

columns.name: HV_DISCR_FREQ_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the discrete frequency record associated with this medication order. This column is frequently used to link to the IP_FREQUENCY table.

columns.name: HV_DISCRETE_DOSE
columns.description: The discrete dose for a medication as entered by the user in the orders activity.

columns.name: HV_DOSE_UNIT_C
columns.description: The category number for the dosage unit of a medication.

columns.name: ORDERING_MODE
columns.description: This column is deprecated. The column previously extracted the category title. In order to avoid data consistency issues, use ORDERING_MODE_C to link to ZC_ORDERING_MODE instead.

columns.name: HV_IS_SELF_ADM_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this medication was self-administered. A Y indicates that the order was self-administered.

columns.name: ORDER_START_TIME
columns.description: The date and time when the medication order is to start. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: ORDER_END_TIME
columns.description: The date and time when the medication order is scheduled to end. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: HV_VERBAL_YN
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for verbal information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: HV_VERBAL_PROV_ID
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for verbal information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: HV_VBL_CSG_USER_ID
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for verbal information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: HV_VBL_MSG_USER_ID
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for verbal information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: VERB_CSGN_TIME
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Refer to tables ORDER_SIGNED_MED and ORDER_SIGNED_PROC for verbal information. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: NON_FORMULARY_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this medication is not on the formulary. A Y indicates a non-formulary medication.

columns.name: CM_PHY_OWNER_ID
columns.description: The Community ID (CID) of the instance that owns this record or line. This is only populated if you use IntraConnect.

columns.name: CM_LOG_OWNER_ID
columns.description: The Community ID (CID) of the instance from which this record or line was extracted. This is only populated if you use IntraConnect.

columns.name: ORDER_STATUS_C
columns.description: The category number for the current status of an order.

columns.name: WORKSTATION_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the workstation record where this order was placed. This column is frequently used to link to the CLARITY_LWS table.

columns.name: AUTHRZING_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider who has prescribed or authorized the medication order. The value in this column matches the value in the MED_PRESC_PROV_ID column.

columns.name: ORD_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider listed as the ordering provider.

columns.name: RX_SESSRPT_DONE_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the order session report has been printed for this medication order. A Y indicates that it has been printed.

columns.name: MIN_DISCRETE_DOSE
columns.description: The minimum ordered dose amount for the medication as specified by the user in the orders activity.

columns.name: MAX_DISCRETE_DOSE
columns.description: The maximum ordered dose amount for the medication as specified by the user in the orders activity.

columns.name: DOSE_UNIT_C
columns.description: The category number for the dose unit of a medication.

columns.name: IS_PENDING_ORD_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the order has a pending status. A Y indicates that the order does have a pending status.

columns.name: BULK_DISP_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this is a bulk dispense order. A Y indicates this is a bulk dispense order.

columns.name: PROVIDER_TYPE_C
columns.description: The medication provider type category number for the order. This item distinguishes between authorizing and documenting provider types for historical and non-historical medications. If the medication was ordered as historical, the provider is considered the documenting provider and is reflected as such in this item. If the medication was not ordered as historical, the provider is considered the authorizing provider is reflected in this item as such.

columns.name: PAT_LOC_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the patient's encounter department at the time of signing the medication order. This column is frequently used to link to the CLARITY_DEP table.

columns.name: MODIFY_TRACK_C
columns.description: The category number for the flag that both indicates and distinguishes whether an order was reordered or modified.

columns.name: SPECIFIED_FIRST_TM
columns.description: If the order was placed with a Specified frequency (the frequency's Type (I EFQ 50) item has a value of 1) and the user specified a first occurrence time, the time specified is stored in this column.

columns.name: SCHED_START_TM
columns.description: The date and time at which an order was scheduled to begin. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: ACT_ORDER_C
columns.description: The category number indicating additional information about an order's status--Active, Completed, Discontinued, or Cancelled. An active order is any order that has not been completed, discontinued, cancelled, pended, or signed and held.

columns.name: PAT_CSN_ID
columns.description: This column is no longer used. Use PAT_ENC_CSN_ID instead.

columns.name: EXP_AFT_START_DATE
columns.description: The number of days after the start date that the medication order will expire based on the setting in the medication record. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: EXP_BEF_END_DATE
columns.description: The number of days before the end date that the medication order will expire based on the setting in the medication record. The date appears in calendar format.

columns.name: MED_COMMENTS
columns.description: Comments for a medication order, as entered by the ordering user when entering the order.

columns.name: USER_SEL_MED_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the orderable medication that is evaluated for Intelligent Medication Selection (IMS). This item is blank if the order is not evaluated for IMS. It is recommended to use the Clarity column ORDER_MEDINFO.DISPENSABLE_MED_ID when reporting on medication orders. Use USER_SEL_MED_ID if searching for medication orders that were evaluated by IMS.

columns.name: USER_SEL_ERX_DAT
columns.description: The date that the medication record was actually selected by the user. This item is populated only if Intelligent Medication Selection (IMS) replaced the original user-selected medication with another medication record.

columns.name: REQ_RNVERIFY_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this medication order requires RN verification before it is administered. A Y indicates that it does require RN verification.

columns.name: MDL_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the medication problem list record that is associated with this medication order. This column is frequently used to link to the MDL_MD_PRBLM_LIST table.

columns.name: LASTDOSE
columns.description: Comments for the last administered dose of a medication entered in the medication documentation navigator section.

columns.name: INFORMANT_C
columns.description: The category number for the informant of a prior to admission (PTA) medication. The informant is the person who reports a PTA medication being taken by the patient.

columns.name: AMB_MED_DISP_NAME
columns.description: The name of the ambulatory medication.

columns.name: MRU_EVAL_RXDISP_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the department record that is associated with the most recent unit evaluated for Rx dispense logic. Most of the time this will store the current unit the patient is in except for the cases where the current unit is set to ignore Rx dispense logic on transfers. This column is frequently used to link to the CLARITY_DEP table.

columns.name: MRCA_EVAL_RXDISP_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the care area that is associated with most recent care area evaluated for Rx dispense logic. This column usually stores the current care area in the patient's department, unless the current unit is set to ignore Rx dispense logic during transfers. This column is frequently used to link to the ED_CARE_AREA_INFO table.

columns.name: CALC_RATE_FRM_VD_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether or not to calculate the rate for this medication order if the volume and duration are specified. The default value is N.

columns.name: WEIGHT_BASED_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the dose for this medication order is based on the patient's weight.

columns.name: WEIGHT_REVIEW_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether or not the patient's weight needs to be reviewed for this medication order due to the patient's weight change.

columns.name: ORD_TM_WEIGHT
columns.description: The patient's last reviewed weight at the time the medication was ordered.

columns.name: ORDER_TIME_WT_INST
columns.description: The date and time when a new weight is recorded for a patient for a weight based medication review.

columns.name: REVIEW_WEIGHT
columns.description: The patient's last non-reviewed weight at the time the medication was ordered.

columns.name: REVIEW_WEIGHT_INST
columns.description: The instant when the patient's last non-reviewed weight was entered prior to when the medication was ordered.

columns.name: REFILLS_REMAINING
columns.description: The number of refills remaining in the medication.

columns.name: MED_REFILL_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider who authorized the medication refill order.

columns.name: OLD_ORDER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the order record that points to the parent medication for refills.

columns.name: OLD_ORDER_DAT
columns.description: The internal contact date of the parent medication in integer format. Used to identify the parent medication and will only be populated for child orders. This does not link to CONTACT_DATE_REAL.

columns.name: RULE_BASED_ORD_T_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this order was routed through rules- based order transmittal. A Y indicates that the order was routed through rules-based order transmittal.

columns.name: RESUME_STATUS_C
columns.description: The category number that indicates an outpatient medication order's status before it was suspended as a result on inpatient admission.

columns.name: USER_ID_OF_PROV
columns.description: The unique ID of the user record that is linked to the provider ID in the AUTHRZING_PROV_ID column.

columns.name: LOGIN_DEP_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the login department record for the user signing the order.

columns.name: SESSION_KEY
columns.description: The session key of this medication order. When a group of orders are signed simultaneously, they share a session key value.

columns.name: ORDERING_MODE_C
columns.description: The category number for the ordering mode of the order (i.e. Outpatient, Inpatient). Note that Outpatient orders can be placed from an Inpatient encounter as discharge orders / take-home prescriptions. This column might be blank for Outpatient orders placed prior to the creation of the IP module.

columns.name: PEND_APPROVE_FLAG_C
columns.description: The pending medication approval status category number for the order.

columns.name: PROV_STATUS_C
columns.description: The provider status category number for the order. This item reflects the providers' viewed status of the order result message. The amount, frequency, and type of data stored in this item are dependent upon programming points that are attached to the results message type definition record used at your facility.

columns.name: NF_POST_VERIF_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether a medication order has been verified by the pharmacist as non-formulary. A Y indicates that the pharmacist verified the medication order as non-formulary. An administrator can use this column to report on how many orders that were placed as non-formulary were also verified as such. To find which orders were placed as non-formulary, use the NON_FORMULARY_YN column.

columns.name: EXT_ELG_SOURCE_ID
columns.description: External eligibility source ID

columns.name: EXT_ELG_MEMBER_ID
columns.description: External eligibility member ID

columns.name: EXT_FORMULARY_ID
columns.description: External formulary ID

columns.name: EXT_COVERAGE_ID
columns.description: External coverage ID

columns.name: EXT_COPAY_ID
columns.description: This column contains the external copay ID for an order.

tables.description: The PATIENT table contains one record for each patient in your system. The data contained in each record consists of demographics, PCP and primary location information, registration information, and other information.

columns.name: PAT_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the patient record for this row. This column is frequently used by other tables to link to PATIENT.

columns.name: PAT_NAME
columns.description: The patient?s name in the format Lastname, Firstname MI.

columns.name: ADD_LINE_1
columns.description: This column, although not deprecated, should no longer be used. Instead you should use the column ADDRESS (EPT 50) in table PAT_ADDRESS. Patient addresses can contain an unlimited number of lines. Previously you could only access the first two lines with the columns PATIENT.ADD_LINE_1 and PATIENT.ADD_LINE_2. Now, there is a new table, called PAT_ADDRESS, which allows you to get all lines of the patient's permanent address. We have chosen not to deprecate PATIENT.ADD_LINE_1 and PATIENT.ADD_LINE_2 because doing so would break any custom reports that use these columns. To use the new column, join column PATIENT.PAT_ID to table PAT_ADDRESS on column PAT_ID and get the ADDRESS value and also the LINE value. Each PAT_ID value represents a different patient, and each LINE value represents a different line of that patient's address.

columns.name: ADD_LINE_2
columns.description: This column, although not deprecated, should no longer be used. Instead you should use the column ADDRESS (EPT 50) in table PAT_ADDRESS. Patient addresses can contain an unlimited number of lines. Previously you could only access the first two lines with the columns PATIENT.ADD_LINE_1 and PATIENT.ADD_LINE_2. Now, there is a new table, called PAT_ADDRESS, which allows you to get all lines of the patient's permanent address. We have chosen not to deprecate PATIENT.ADD_LINE_1 and PATIENT.ADD_LINE_2 because doing so would break any custom reports that use these columns. To use the new column, join column PATIENT.PAT_ID to table PAT_ADDRESS on column PAT_ID and get the ADDRESS value and also the LINE value. Each PAT_ID value represents a different patient, and each LINE value represents a different line of that patient's address.

tables.description: The patient encounter table contains one record for each patient encounter in your system. By default, this table does not contain Registration or PCP/Clinic Change contacts (encounter types 1 and 31). It does contain all appointments, office visits, telephone encounters, and other types of encounters. The primary key for the patient encounter table is PAT_ENC_CSN_ID. Note that there is an index named EIX_FILT_PAT_ENC_RFL on the REFERRAL_ID column in Oracle that does not appear in the index list. The index is created by EFN_FAUX_RFL_FILT_INX.

columns.name: PAT_ID
columns.description: The unique ID assigned to the patient record. This ID may be encrypted if you have elected to use enterprise reporting?s encryption utility.

columns.name: PAT_ENC_DATE_REAL
columns.description: A unique contact date in decimal format. The integer portion of the number indicates the date of contact. The digits after the decimal distinguish different contacts on the same date and are unique for each contact on that date. For example, .00 is the first/only contact, .01 is the second contact, etc.

columns.name: PAT_ENC_CSN_ID
columns.description: A unique serial number for this encounter. This number is unique across all patients and encounters in the system.

columns.name: CONTACT_DATE
columns.description: The date of this contact in calendar format.

columns.name: ENC_TYPE_C
columns.description: The encounter type category number for the encounter.

columns.name: ENC_TYPE_TITLE
columns.description: This column is deprecated. The column previously extracted the category title. In order to avoid data consistency issues, use ENC_TYPE_C to link to ZC_DISP_ENC_TYPE instead.

columns.name: AGE
columns.description: In table PAT_ENC, the column AGE has been deprecated. To look up the deprecated column's value after the Clarity Compass upgrade, join column PAT_ENC.PAT_ID to PATIENT.PAT_ID, and calculate the difference between PAT_ENC.CONTACT_DATE and PATIENT.BIRTH_DATE. In many cases, the CONTACT_DATE column that the AGE column was based on will not be the most relevant date for the contact. For example for an admission, you may wish to join PATIENT.PAT_ID to PAT_ENC_HSP.PAT_ID and calculate the difference between PAT_ENC_HSP.HOSP_ADMSN_TIME or PAT_ENC_HSP.HOSP_DISCH_TIME and PATIENT.BIRTH_DATE.

columns.name: PCP_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider record for the patient?s General Primary Care Provider as of the date of the encounter. This ID may be encrypted if you have elected to use enterprise reporting?s security utility.

columns.name: FIN_CLASS_C
columns.description: The category value associated with the Financial Class of the encounter. Note: This item is only populated through an interface. It is not populated if you have billing system installed.

columns.name: VISIT_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID for the visit provider associated with this encounter. In cases where there are multiple providers for one encounter, this is the ID of the first provider in the list. This item may be NULL if there is no provider for this encounter. This ID may be encrypted.

columns.name: VISIT_PROV_TITLE
columns.description: The visit provider?s provider title (SER 5). See VISIT_PROV_ID above for the definition of visit provider.

columns.name: DEPARTMENT_ID
columns.description: The ID of the department for the encounter. If there are multiple departments for the encounter, this is the ID of the first department in the list.

columns.name: BP_SYSTOLIC
columns.description: The systolic portion of the patient?s blood pressure measured at this encounter.

columns.name: BP_DIASTOLIC
columns.description: The diastolic portion of the patient?s blood pressure measured at this encounter.

columns.name: TEMPERATURE
columns.description: The patient?s temperature taken during this encounter. This value is in degrees Fahrenheit regardless of how the temperature reading was entered.

columns.name: PULSE
columns.description: The patient?s pulse taken during this encounter.

columns.name: WEIGHT
columns.description: The patient?s weight as recorded during this encounter. Note: This field is stored in ounces regardless of how the weight was entered. Divide this number by 16 to report the patient?s weight in pounds.

columns.name: HEIGHT
columns.description: The patient?s height as recorded during this encounter. This field is a string and may contain indicators for feet and/or inches.

columns.name: RESPIRATIONS
columns.description: The patient?s respiration rate as recorded during this encounter.

columns.name: LMP_DATE
columns.description: The date of the patient?s Last Menstrual Period. Only contains data for encounters with female patients.

columns.name: LMP_OTHER_C
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** In table PAT_ENC, the column LMP_OTHER_C (EPT/17901) has been deprecated. This column has been replaced by column OBGYN_STAT_C (EPT/18940) in the table V_OB_ENC_OBGYN_STAT. To look up the deprecated column's value after the Clarity Compass upgrade, use the column OBGYN_STAT_C in table V_OB_ENC_OBGYN_STAT to get the value.

columns.name: HEAD_CIRCUMFERENCE
columns.description: The patient?s Head Circumference as recorded during this encounter. This item will contain data only for patients younger than the age specified in Miscellaneous Configuration. Note: This value is stored in centimeters.

columns.name: ENC_CLOSED_YN
columns.description: A flag that signifies if this encounter is closed as of the time of the enterprise reporting extract. This column will have the value Y, N or null. Null indicates that closing the encounter does not apply, such as a future appointment.

columns.name: ENC_CLOSED_USER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the system user who closed the patient encounter. This ID may be encrypted.

columns.name: ENC_CLOSE_DATE
columns.description: The date on which the patient encounter was closed.

columns.name: LOS_PRIME_PROC_ID
columns.description: The ID of the procedure record corresponding to the primary LOS code for this encounter. Note: This is not the CPT? code. It is an internal identifier that is typically not visible to a user.

columns.name: LOS_PROC_CODE
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** This column has been deprecated. Please use PAT_ENC.LOS_PRIME_PROC_ID to join to CLARITY_EAP to retrieve this data. The procedure code for the primary LOS (level of service).

columns.name: LOS_MODIFIER1_ID
columns.description: The first Level of Service modifier applied to the encounter. This item will appear empty if no modifier is present.

columns.name: LOS_MODIFIER2_ID
columns.description: The second Level of Service modifier applied to the encounter. This item will appear empty if no modifier is present.

columns.name: LOS_MODIFIER3_ID
columns.description: The third Level of Service modifier applied to the encounter. This item will appear empty if no modifier is present.

columns.name: LOS_MODIFIER4_ID
columns.description: The fourth Level of Service modifier applied to the encounter. This item will appear empty if no modifier is present.

columns.name: CHKIN_INDICATOR_C
columns.description: The visit indicator category number for this patient encounter.

columns.name: CHKIN_INDICATOR_DT
columns.description: The date associated with the visit indicator as entered by an system user at Check In.

columns.name: APPT_STATUS_C
columns.description: The category value associated with the appointment status of the encounter as of the most recent enterprise reporting extract, such as 1 ? Scheduled, 2 ? Completed, 3 ? Canceled, etc.

columns.name: APPT_BLOCK_C
columns.description: The scheduling system block type category number for the patient encounter.

columns.name: APPT_TIME
columns.description: The scheduled appointment date and time for the encounter recorded using a twenty-four hour clock, i.e. 1 P.M. on January 4, 2000, would be 01/04/2000 13:00.

columns.name: APPT_LENGTH
columns.description: Scheduled appointment length in minutes.

columns.name: APPT_MADE_DATE
columns.description: The date on which the appointment was made. Note that this may be updated if the appointment is changed. To determine the date on which the appointment was first entered into the system, use the column APPT_MADE_DATE in the derived table F_SCHED_APPT, or join to the audit trail table PAT_ENC_ES_AUD_ACT where LINE = 1.

columns.name: APPT_PRC_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the visit type (PRC .1) assigned to the encounter when the appointment is made.

columns.name: CHECKIN_TIME
columns.description: The date and time the patient was checked in for the appointment for this encounter. Note that the date portion of this value is always the appointment date, regardless of the date on which the appointment was actually checked in. Also, the time portion of this value can be modified by users in the application. This may or may not be more accurate than the system-audited time. To determine the check in instant recorded by the system, use the CHECKIN_AUD_DTTM column in PAT_ENC_7.

columns.name: CHECKOUT_TIME
columns.description: The date and time the patient was checked out for this encounter. Note that the date portion of this value is always the appointment date, regardless of the date on which the appointment was actually checked out. Also, the time portion of this value can be modified by users in the application. This may or may not be more accurate than the system-audited time. To determine the check out instant recorded by the system, use the CHECKOUT_AUD_DTTM column in PAT_ENC_7.

columns.name: ARVL_LST_DL_TIME
columns.description: The date and time the encounter was deleted from the arrival list. NOTE: This column will try to pull from the ARRIVAL LIST DELETE WHEN item (EPT 7233) first, then if it finds nothing there it will pull from AUDITED INSTANT (EPT 7544).

columns.name: ARVL_LST_DL_USR_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the system user who removed the encounter from the arrival list. This ID may be encrypted. NOTE: This column will try to pull from the ARRIVAL LIST DELETE BY WHOM item (EPT 7234) first, then if it finds nothing there it will pull from AUDITED USER ID (EPT 7543).

columns.name: APPT_ENTRY_USER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the system user who entered the appointment. Note that this may be updated if the appointment is changed. To determine the user who first entered the appointment into the system, use the column APPT_ENTRY_USER_ID in the derived table F_SCHED_APPT, or join to the audit trail table PAT_ENC_ES_AUD_ACT where LINE = 1.

columns.name: APPT_CANC_USER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the user who canceled the appointment.

columns.name: APPT_CANCEL_DATE
columns.description: The date the appointment was canceled. If the appointment was not canceled this field is NULL.

columns.name: CHECKIN_USER_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the system user who checked in the patient for this encounter. If the encounter has not been through the Check In process this field will be NULL. This ID may be encrypted.

columns.name: CANCEL_REASON_C
columns.description: The cancel reason category number for the patient encounter.

columns.name: APPT_SERIAL_NO
columns.description: A non-unique serial number for the appointment. The APPT_SERIAL_NO is the same as the PAT_ENC_CSN_ID unless the appointment is canceled and rescheduled. In that case, the APPT_SERIAL_NO for the rescheduled appointment is the same as the initial appointment; however, these appointments have different PAT_ENC_CSN_IDs.

columns.name: HOSP_ADMSN_TIME
columns.description: The date and time that the patient was first admitted to the facility, bedded in the ED, or confirmed for an HOV for this contact, regardless of patient's base patient class.

columns.name: HOSP_DISCHRG_TIME
columns.description: The hospital discharge date and time for this patient contact.

columns.name: HOSP_ADMSN_TYPE_C
columns.description: The category value for the type of admission for this encounter.

columns.name: NONCVRED_SERVICE_YN
columns.description: A flag used to indicate whether the appointment is scheduled in a service not covered by the patient's coverage benefits. The flag is set to "Y" when the service is not covered and an "N" when it is covered.

columns.name: REFERRAL_REQ_YN
columns.description: A flag used to indicate whether an appointment requires a referral as determined by the visit coverage. This flag is set to ?Y? when the appointment requires a referral. If the appointment does not require a referral, it is set to ?N."

columns.name: REFERRAL_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the referral record linked to this appointment.

columns.name: ACCOUNT_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the guarantor account assigned to the visit at the time it is scheduled or when it is checked in. This ID may be encrypted.

columns.name: COVERAGE_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the coverage record assigned to the visit at the time it is scheduled or when it is checked in. This ID may be encrypted.

columns.name: AR_EPISODE_ID
columns.description: In the table PAT_ENC, the column AR_EPISODE_ID (EPT,2208) has been deprecated. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles.

columns.name: CLAIM_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the billing system Claim record (CLM record) linked to charges associated with this visit.

columns.name: PRIMARY_LOC_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the patient?s primary location as of the contact date of the encounter. Note: This may not be the same as the patient?s current primary location.

columns.name: CHARGE_SLIP_NUMBER
columns.description: The encounter form number or charge slip number assigned to this encounter. Note: The charge slip number is also stored in the financial table CLARITY_TDL. You can use this field to link to CLARITY_TDL to identify financial transactions associated with the encounter.

columns.name: VISIT_EPM_ID
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** This column has been deprecated. Please use PAT_ENC.COVERAGE_ID to join to V_COVERAGE_PAYOR_PLAN and filter on EFF_DATE and TERM_DATE to find the relevant PAYOR_ID. The unique ID of the payor associated with the visit coverage.

columns.name: VISIT_EPP_ID
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** This column has been deprecated. Please use PAT_ENC.COVERAGE_ID to join to V_COVERAGE_PAYOR_PLAN and filter on EFF_DATE and TERM_DATE to find the relevant BENEFIT_PLAN_ID. The unique ID of the benefit plan associated with the visit coverage.

columns.name: COPAY_DUE
columns.description: The dollar amount shown in the Copay Due field of the scheduling system's Check In Patient activity. This amount may be calculated by the system using the patient's coverage benefit information or be manually entered by a user. This field may also be empty if no copay amount was entered when the patient's appointment was checked in.

columns.name: COPAY_COLLECTED
columns.description: The amount in the Copay Collected field entered by the user during Check In. Note: If your facility uses the Registration or POS copay form at check-in/check-out, users may collect more than one copay per appointment. The value in this field only reflects the last copay collected for any given appointment. Data for all copays collected can be found in the PAT_ENC_COPAY_COLL table. If your facility uses the AR copay form, this is the correct data source to use.

columns.name: COPAY_SOURCE_C
columns.description: The copay source category number for the patient encounter. Note: If your facility uses the Registration or POS copay form at check-in/check-out, users may collect more than one copay per appointment. The value in this field only reflects the last copay collected for any given appointment. Data for all copays collected can be found in the PAT_ENC_COPAY_COLL table. If your facility uses the AR copay form, this is the correct data source to use.

columns.name: COPAY_TYPE_C
columns.description: The copay type category number of the patient encounter. Note: If your facility uses the Registration or POS copay form at check-in/check-out, users may collect more than one copay per appointment. The value in this field only reflects the last copay collected for any given appointment. Data for all copays collected can be found in the PAT_ENC_COPAY_COLL table. If your facility uses the AR copay form, this is the correct data source to use.

columns.name: COPAY_REF_NUM
columns.description: The reference number of the copay as recorded for this encounter during Check In. This could be the check number or credit card number for the copay. Note: If your facility uses the Registration or POS copay form at check-in/check-out, users may collect more than one copay per appointment. The value in this field only reflects the last copay collected for any given appointment. Data for all copays collected can be found in the PAT_ENC_COPAY_COLL table. If your facility uses the AR copay form, this is the correct data source to use.

columns.name: COPAY_PMT_EXPL_C
columns.description: The copay payment explanation category number of this patient encounter. Note: This field is only populated with data if your facility uses the POS copay form at check-in/check-out. Users may collect more than one copay per appointment. The value in this field only reflects the last copay collected for any given appointment. Data for all copays collected can be found in the PAT_ENC_COPAY_COLL table.

columns.name: UPDATE_DATE
columns.description: The time this patient encounter was pulled into enterprise reporting.

columns.name: SERV_AREA_ID
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** The function used to extract this data is no longer used. ****** The link to the Service Area for the Encounter

columns.name: HSP_ACCOUNT_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the hospital billing account assigned to the encounter.

columns.name: ADM_FOR_SURG_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the patient is being admitted for surgery.

columns.name: SURGICAL_SVC_C
columns.description: The category value corresponding to the surgical service for this patient contact.

columns.name: INPATIENT_DATA_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the record used to determine how inpatient data is stored for the encounter.

columns.name: IP_EPISODE_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the inpatient episode of care. This includes discharges from the ED.

columns.name: APPT_QNR_ANS_ID
columns.description: This column is deprecated and does not extract any data. To report on answers to appointment questionnaires, use the table PAT_ENC_QNRS_ANS.

columns.name: ATTND_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the attending provider.

columns.name: ORDERING_PROV_TEXT
columns.description: Data from a free text field that contains information on the provider who placed the order.

columns.name: ES_ORDER_STATUS_C
columns.description: Scheduling system order status.

columns.name: EXTERNAL_VISIT_ID
columns.description: The ID for the contact as assigned by a non-system. Usually populated by an interface.

columns.name: CONTACT_COMMENT

tables.description: This table is the primary table for hospital encounter information. A hospital encounter is a contact in the patient record created through an ADT workflow such as preadmission, admission, ED Arrival, discharge, and hospital outpatient visit (HOV) contacts. columns.description: *** Deprecated *** In the table PAT_ENC_HSP, the column PAT_CONTACT_MPI_NO has been deprecated. The patient's MRN, of the type associated with the location for this contact. The MRN displayed is not guaranteed to be current. In particular, it can get out of date if the patient's MRN is manually edited or if the contact location is updated to use a different MRN type. For this reason, it is highly recommended that you use a different column to display the patient's MRN. Several columns are available. To continue to display the location-specific MRN, join to table CLARITY_DEP by setting PAT_ENC_HSP.DEPARTMENT_ID = CLARITY_DEP.DEPARTMENT_ID; then join to table V_PAT_EAF_MRN by setting PAT_ENC_HSP.PAT_ID = V_PAT_EAF_MRN.PAT_ID and CLARITY_DEP.REV_LOC_ID = V_PAT_EAF_MRN.EAF_ID; and finally, display column V_PAT_EAF_MRN.PAT_MRN. If you prefer to display the department-specific MRN, simply join to table V_PAT_DEP_MRN by setting PAT_ENC_HSP.PAT_ID = V_PAT_DEP_MRN.PAT_ID and PAT_ENC_HSP.DEPARTMENT_ID = V_PAT_DEP_MRN.DEP_ID; then, display column V_PAT_DEP_MRN.PAT_MRN. Similarly, if you prefer to display the service area-specific MRN, join to table V_PAT_EAF_MRN by setting PAT_ENC_HSP.PAT_ID = V_PAT_EAF_MRN.PAT_ID and PAT_ENC_HSP.ADT_SERV_AREA_ID = V_PAT_EAF_MRN.EAF_ID; then, display column V_PAT_EAF_MRN.PAT_MRN. If, instead, you want to display an MRN of a specific type (such as your facility-wide MRN type), join to table IDENTITY_ID by setting column PAT_ENC_HSP.PAT_ID equal to column IDENTITY_ID.PAT_ID, filter on column IDENTITY_ID.IDENTITY_ID_TYPE, and display column IDENTITY_ID.IDENTITY_ID. Another option is to join to PATIENT by setting PAT_ENC_HSP.PAT_ID = PATIENT.PAT_ID and to display column PATIENT.PAT_MRN_ID. For details on how PATIENT.PAT_MRN_ID determines the patient's MRN, see the column description for table PATIENT column PAT_MRN_ID.

columns.description: Indicates the delivery method of the last baby delivered on this encounter by this patient. For example, Spontaneous Vaginal Delivery, C-Section - Unspecified, etc.

columns.name: PRENATAL_CARE_C
columns.description: Item used to indicate what type of prenatal care the patient has received.

columns.name: AMBULANCE_CODE_C
columns.description: The category number for the ambulance code.

columns.name: MSE_DATE
columns.description: Indicates the date and time of the patient's medical screening exam (MSE).

columns.name: ADMIT_PROV_TEXT
columns.description: The free text admitting provider for the encounter.

columns.name: ATTEND_PROV_TEXT
columns.description: The free text attending provider for the encounter.

columns.name: PROV_PRIM_TEXT
columns.description: The free text primary care provider for the encounter

columns.name: PROV_PRIM_TEXT_PHON
columns.description: The free text phone number for the primary care provider.

columns.name: HOSPITAL_AREA_ID
columns.description: This field identifies the hospital area associated with the hospital unit in this patient contact.

columns.name: ADMIT_ADDR_ID
columns.description: Address ID for attending provider (EPT 18867)

columns.name: CHIEF_COMPLAINT_C
columns.description: This holds the category number for the chief complaint if the free text chief complaint item is not being used.

columns.name: NEED_FIN_CLR_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether the patient requires financial clearance on this encounter.

columns.name: MU_HOSP_ADMSN_TIME
columns.description: The date and time the patient was admitted to inpatient or roomed in ED. It will look at item EPT 10290 to see if it is populated. If yes, it will be populated with the instant the patient was first inpatient (EPT 10290 and 10291). Otherwise, it will look at which version of the standard unique patient denominator is being used (LSD 34062) and whether the patient was an ED patient (EPT 10296 and 10297). If the standard unique patient denominator being used in LSD 34062 is the all ED patients denominator, this item will be populated with the hospital admission date and time (EPT 18850 and 18851). If the patient was not inpatient and not seen in the ED, this item will be null. If the standard unique patient denominator being using in LSD 34062 is the ED to Observation patients only denominator, if the patient was seen in the ED and was an observation patient, this item will be populated with the hospital admission date and time (EPT 18850 and 18851). If the settings for excluding newborns are present and the encounter is for a newborn, this item will be null.

tables.description: The REFERRAL table is the primary table for referral information stored in system.

columns.name: REFERRAL_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the referral in database. This is the primary key for the REFERRAL table.

columns.name: EXTERNAL_ID_NUM
columns.description: The external identification number used on the referral.

columns.name: PAT_ID
columns.description: The ID of the patient associated with the referral.

columns.name: PCP_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the patient's primary care provider at the time the referral was created.

columns.name: ENTRY_DATE
columns.description: The date the referral was entered.

columns.name: RFL_STATUS_C
columns.description: The category value representing the status of the referral (I.e. authorized, open, pending, etc.).

columns.name: REFERRING_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the referral source (REF) record of the provider who made the referral. This column is frequently used to link to the REFERRAL_SOURCE table. The actual provider (SER) ID can be found in column REF_PROVIDER_ID of table REFERRAL_SOURCE.

columns.name: VENDOR_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the vendor associated with the referral.

columns.name: REFERRAL_PROV_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the provider (SER) being referred to. This column is frequently used to link to the CLARITY_SER table.

columns.name: PROV_SPEC_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the provider specialty being referred to.

columns.name: RFL_TYPE_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the type of referral.

columns.name: RSN_FOR_RFL_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the main (first) reason for the referral. Since multiple reasons can be listed, use table REFERRAL_REASONS to view all of them.

columns.name: RFL_CLASS_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the class of the referral.

columns.name: AUTH_VIS_PERIOD
columns.description: The number of authorized visits in each visit period.

columns.name: AUTH_PERIOD_TYPE_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the type of period for authorized visits - i.e. hour, day, week, month, year.

columns.name: AUTH_NUM_PERIODS
columns.description: The number of periods authorized for this referral.

columns.name: AUTH_NUM_OF_VISITS
columns.description: The number of visits authorized for this referral.

columns.name: ADMISSION_DATE
columns.description: The admission date associated with the referral.

columns.name: DISCHARGE_DATE
columns.description: The discharge date associated with the referral.

columns.name: ESTIMATED_DAYS
columns.description: The authorized length of stay if the patient is being admitted.

columns.name: OVERRIDE_DAYS
columns.description: The actual length of stay. This column is deprecated as "Length of Stay" has been renamed as "Bed Days". The data can now be found in I RFL 2074.

columns.name: START_DATE
columns.description: The start date of the referral.

columns.name: EXP_DATE
columns.description: The expiration date of the referral.

columns.name: PEND_TO
columns.description: The person or pool to whom an In Basket message should be sent about this referral.

columns.name: PEND_RSN_C
columns.description: For pended referrals, the category value indicating the reason for pending.

columns.name: DENY_RSN_C
columns.description: For denied referrals, the category value indicating the reason for denial.

columns.name: SERV_AREA_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the service area associated with the referral.

columns.name: COVERAGE_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the coverage associated with the referral.

columns.name: NUM_PROC
columns.description: The number of procedures associated with the referral.

columns.name: SVC_DATE_REAL
columns.description: If available, this column is populated by the authorized start date (I RFL 85). If not, it is populated by the expiration date on the referral (I RFL 90). If neither of these are available, the column will be empty. The date in this column is based on days since December 31, 1840.

columns.name: CARRIER_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the carrier associated with the referral.

columns.name: PAYOR_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the payor associated with the referral.

columns.name: PLAN_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the plan associated with the referral.

columns.name: SERV_DATE
columns.description: This column is populated by the authorized start date (I RFL 85) if available. If not, it is populated by the expiration date on the referral (I RFL 90). If neither of these are available, the column will be empty. The date in this column is in MM/DD/YYYY format.

columns.name: RETRO_FLAG_YN
columns.description: The category value used to mark a referral as being "Retro" entered.

columns.name: IBNR
columns.description: The "Incurred but not reported" amount associated with this referral.

columns.name: AUTO_APPROVED_DATE
columns.description: The date on which the referral was approved automatically by the system.

columns.name: AUTH_RSN_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the authorization reason associated with the referral.

columns.name: REFD_BY_LOC_POS_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the place of service the referral was referred from.

columns.name: REFD_TO_LOC_POS_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the place of service the referral was referred to.

columns.name: REFD_TO_DEPT_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the department the referral was referred to.

columns.name: REFD_TO_SPEC_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the specialty the department was referred to.

columns.name: PRIORITY_C
columns.description: The category value indicating the priority of the referral.

columns.name: TOTAL_PRICE
columns.description: The total cost of the procedures authorized under the referral.

columns.name: TOTAL_PAYABLE
columns.description: The portion of the total price for which your facility is responsible.

columns.name: PATIENT_AMOUNT
columns.description: The total patient liability, under the parameters of the primary coverage used, for the procedures authorized under the referral.

columns.name: EXPECT_TO_PAY
columns.description: The total amount you expect your facility will pay for the procedures authorized under the referral. This amount entered by you overrides the total payable amount for the purpose of calculating IBNR.

columns.name: IBNR_PAY_UNTIL_DT
columns.description: The date up to which your facility will pay claims for the procedures approved on this referral.

columns.name: CASE_RATE_YN
columns.description: The category value indicating whether this referral involves services that are reimbursed at a specific case rate.

columns.name: PRIM_LOC_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the member's primary location at the time the referral was entered.

columns.name: MED_TYPE_C
columns.description: *** Deprecated *** In table REFERRAL, the column MED_TYPE_C (RFL/18016) has been deprecated. The deprecated column's content/data is no longer available since it is no longer populated in Chronicles. ****** The category value indicating what type of confirmation it is - acute, chronic or PAT.

columns.name: ACUTE_AMOUNT
columns.description: The amount of the confirmation if the confirmation is acute.

columns.name: CHRONIC_AMOUNT
columns.description: The amount of the confirmation if the confirmation is chronic.

columns.name: PAT_AMOUNT
columns.description: The amount of the confirmation if the confirmation is an acute medication that was suggested by the pharmacist or over-the-counter medication.

columns.name: UPDATE_DATE
columns.description: The instant of time when the referral was last updated.

columns.name: RFL_LOB_ID
columns.description: ID of the Line of Business (LOB) assigned to the referral.

columns.name: ACTUAL_NUM_VISITS
columns.description: The actual number of completed visits for this referral.

columns.name: SCHED_NUM_VISITS
columns.description: The number of visits scheduled for this referral.

columns.name: REQUEST_NUM_VISITS
columns.description: The number of visits requested for this referral.

columns.name: GUIDELINE_DAYS
columns.description: Guideline days for this referral.

columns.name: OVRD_ADMIT_DATE
columns.description: Override admit date for this referral.

columns.name: OVRD_DISCHARGE_DT
columns.description: Override discharge date for this referral.

columns.name: DISP_VAL_C
columns.description: Whether the referral was accepted ("appointed") or refused ("denied") by the referred-to provider, department or facility.

columns.name: DISP_RSN_C
columns.description: The reason that a referral's disposition has a status of "denied."

columns.name: DISP_EAF_ID
columns.description: The unique id of the facility to which the referral was forwarded.

columns.name: CM_PHY_OWNER_ID
columns.description: ID of the physical deployment owner for this record. Physical owners will be where the data is hosted, either on the cross-over server or the owner deployment.

columns.name: CM_LOG_OWNER_ID
columns.description: ID of the logical deployment owner for this record. Logical owners show the deployment where the record was created, but don't represent the logical owner if the record is a part of the version skew.

columns.name: REFD_BY_DEPT_ID
columns.description: The ID number of the department the referral was referred by.

columns.name: CLOSE_RSN_C
columns.description: For closed referrals, the category value indicating the reason for closing.

columns.name: RECORD_STATUS_C
columns.description: Record status. Possible values: null = normal record 4 = "hidden" record, i.e. voided. For the category list, use: ZC_RECORD_STATUS_2

columns.name: SCHED_STATUS_C
columns.description: Scheduling status of the referral to keep track of internally schedulable referrals. For category list use ZC_SCHED_STATUS.

columns.name: SCHED_BY_DATE
columns.description: Indicates deadline to schedule a referral.

columns.name: PREAUTH_REQ_C
columns.description: For referrals created from an order, indicates if a preauthorization number must be collected before scheduling.

columns.name: NOT_COLLCTD_RSN_C
columns.description: Reason indicating why the preauth number will not be collected for this referral.

columns.name: PREAUTH_DEFER_DT
columns.description: Set by the user to indicate they don't want to see the record until that date on the preauthorization workqueue report.

columns.name: PROCESSED_RSN_C
columns.description: Reason indicating why the preauth number is marked as processed for this referral.

columns.name: PREAUTH_CHG_EMP_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the user who last changed the preauthorization data.

columns.name: PREAUTH_CHNGD_DTTM
columns.description: Date/time stamp for last time the preauthorization data was changed.

columns.name: AUTH_NUM
columns.description: Authorization number.

columns.name: PRE_CERT_NUM
columns.description: Pre-certification number.

columns.name: NON_PREF_PROV_RSN_C
columns.description: Stores the reason why a non preferred level provider was chosen.

columns.name: EXT_REF_DATE
columns.description: This is the external referring date.

columns.name: EOW_ID
columns.description: The unique EOW ID associated with the referral.

columns.name: IB_STATUS_EXPLAN
columns.description: The IB status explanation for the referral.

columns.name: REQ_VIS_PER_PERIOD
columns.description: The requested visits per period on the referral.

columns.name: REQ_PERIOD_TYPE_C
columns.description: The requested period type on the referral.

columns.name: REQ_NUM_OF_PERIODS
columns.description: The requested number of periods on the referral.

columns.name: REF_FROM_ECI_ID
columns.description: Used by Chart Sync to determine the referring deployment, so that a "ping pong" message can be sent to them.

columns.name: PREV_REF_FRM_ECI_ID
columns.description: Used by Chart Sync to determine the previously referring deployment.

columns.name: EOW_ON_ECI_ID
columns.description: The unique ID of the deployment where the referral verification message should be generated. This is used for Cross Deployment messaging.

columns.name: REF_TO_PROV_ADDR_ID
columns.description: This stores the address ID of the referred to provider. The format is as follows: ProvID-AddressID. AddressID is the line number of the multiple response address items in the SER masterfile. To use this column, join to CLARITY_SER_ADDR on REFERRAL.REF_TO_PROV_ADDR_ID = CLARITY_SER_ADDR.ADDR_UNIQUE_ID. If you use IntraConnect, also join on REFERRAL.REFERRAL_PROV_ID = CLARITY_SER_ADDR.PROV_ID.

columns.name: REF_TO_ECI_ID
columns.description: Used by Chart Sync to determine the referred to deployment.

columns.name: NUM_CLMS_EXPECTED
columns.description: Number of claims expected to be filed on this referral.

columns.name: RFL_STATCHG_RSN_C
columns.description: The reason category number describing why the referral status was changed.

columns.name: RFL_SENS_C
columns.description: The sensitivity category number for the referral.

columns.name: TOTAL_EST_DAYS
columns.description: Total estimated days for the referral.

columns.name: TOTAL_OVERRIDE_DAYS
columns.description: The total number of override days on the referral.

columns.name: TOTAL_CONVTD_DAYS
columns.description: The total number of converted days on the referral.

columns.name: AMT_CLMS_ADJUDICTD
columns.description: The amount of claims adjudicated.

columns.name: AMT_CLMS_PAID
columns.description: The amount of claims paid.

columns.name: ADJ_VENDOR_ID
columns.description: The adjudication vendor.

columns.name: ADJ_MEMBER_GROUP_ID
columns.description: Adjudication member group.

columns.name: ADJ_NET_STATUS_C
columns.description: Adjudication network status.

columns.name: NO_CLAIMS_PAID
columns.description: The number of claims paid on the claim.

columns.name: CVG_REFRESH_DATE
columns.description: Coverage refresh date.

columns.name: ENTRY_SOURCE_C
columns.description: Entry source of the referral.

columns.name: IS_COPY
columns.description: Indicates whether this referral was created via a copy workflow. Note that this extracts the value of 1 rather than a Y. 0 or null indicate that the referral was not created via a copy workflow.

columns.name: SUPPRESS_EXP_WAR_YN
columns.description: Indicates whether this referral will be skipped when evaluating if an expiring referral warning should be sent.

columns.name: ADJUD_SERV_AREA_ID
columns.description: Service area used in referral pricing and adjudication.

columns.name: PREV_REF_TO_ECI_ID
columns.description: Used by Chart Sync to determine the previously referred to deployment, so that a ping pong message can be sent to them.
